_description: Canonical tracker features for each Codex of Embodied Arcana page. Deploy to ~/syric_arcane_controller/ alongside tracker_sync.py. Values hard-coded for Syric at level 13 (tier L11-16), except Arcane Charge which should track Syric's Arcana bonus. Update max values manually when leveling into a new tier. Codex Dice migrates from the old Burn Dice key for existing live trackers.

_level_notes.Echoed Rune / Veil: half proficiency bonus rounded down (PB5 at L13 = 2 uses)
_level_notes.Resonance Points: half wizard level rounded up + INT mod (L13 INT20 = 7+5 = 12)
_level_notes.Arcane Charge: Max and overload threshold equal Syric's Arcana bonus. L13 Arcana bonus = 10, so max = 10.

1.title: The First Rune — Awakening the Arcane Flesh

features.name: Overload System (Codex P2)
features.description:
Inscription — Binding Magic to Flesh (Page 2). PASSIVE SYSTEM — tap to reference.

INSCRIBING A RUNE
Time: 1 hour per spell level. Cantrips: 30 minutes.
Required Check: Arcana check. DC = 10 + spell level.
Success: Spell inscribed as a rune.
Failure: Rune is unstable, cannot be used until long rest.

ARCANE CHARGE
Each time Syric casts a leveled spell, he gains Arcane Charge equal to the spell's level. Shadow-cast spells using Syric's slots count. Reaction spells expending a leveled slot count normally. Charge resets to 0 when it naturally dissipates, is fully vented, or Arcane Discharge occurs.

ARCANE CAPACITY (THRESHOLD): Equal to Syric's Arcana Bonus (10 at L13). Dynamic — updates automatically.
Charge CAN exceed the threshold — the threshold is when consequences begin, not a hard cap.

NATURAL DISSIPATION
End of turn: auto-reduce by minimum possible Vent Die value (2 at L11-16 with 2d6).
If no leveled spell cast that round: fully discharge at max Vent Die value (12 at L11-16 with 2d6).

VENT DIE SCALING: L5-10 = 1d6 | L11-16 = 2d6 | L17-20 = 3d6

OVERLOAD STATE TRIGGER
Arcane Charge equals or exceeds Arcane Capacity (Arcana Bonus). Entering Overload State does NOT immediately force a check.
Once in Overload State, casting another leveled spell triggers an Overload Check.

OVERLOAD CHECK
Arcana check. DC = current Arcane Charge total.
SUCCESS: Maintain control. Remain in Overload State.
FAILURE: Arcane Discharge occurs.

OVERLOAD STATE ENDS
If Arcane Charge drops below Arcane Capacity, Overload ends immediately. Charge is retained.

ARCANE DISCHARGE (on failed Overload Check)
20-foot radius centered on Syric. Damage = 2x stored spell levels (force).
DEX save for half (DC = Syric's spell save DC). Syric auto-fails, takes full damage.
After Effect: Roll Arcane Rebound d100 table. Charge resets to 0.

ARCANE VENTING (Bonus Action)
Roll Vent Die (2d6 at L11-16), reduce Arcane Charge by result. Min charge 0.

END OF TURN INSTABILITY
After passive discharge, if charge still >= Arcane Capacity, must immediately make Overload Check.

item_charges.name: Arcane Charge
item_charges.description:
Inscription (Page 2). BUILDING COUNTER — auto-increments when spell slots are spent.

Gains Arcane Charge equal to spell level cast. Shadow-cast spells using Syric's slots also count. Reaction spells that expend a leveled slot count normally.

ARCANE CAPACITY (THRESHOLD): Arcana Bonus (10 at L13). Dynamic — recalculates when Tracker tab redraws.
Charge CAN exceed this threshold — it marks when Overload State begins, not a hard cap.

OVERLOAD STATE: Triggered when charge equals or exceeds Arcane Capacity. Does NOT immediately force a check.
While in Overload State, casting another leveled spell immediately triggers an Overload Check.

OVERLOAD CHECK: Arcana check. DC = 10 + current Arcane Charge total.
SUCCESS: Maintain control. Remain in Overload State.
FAILURE: Arcane Discharge occurs.

OVERLOAD STATE ENDS: If charge drops below Arcane Capacity, Overload ends immediately. Charge is retained.

ARCANE DISCHARGE (on failed check):
20-foot radius centered on Syric. Damage = 2x stored spell levels (force).
DEX save for half (DC = Syric's spell save DC). Syric auto-fails, takes full damage.
After Effect: Roll Arcane Rebound d100 table. Charge resets to 0.

ARCANE VENTING (Bonus Action): Roll Vent Die (2d6 at L11-16), subtract result from charge. Min charge 0.
PASSIVE DISCHARGE (end of turn): Reduce by minimum Vent Die value (2 at L11-16).
MAX DISSIPATION: If no leveled spell cast that round, discharge at max Vent Die value (12 at L11-16).
END OF TURN INSTABILITY: After passive discharge, if charge still >= threshold, immediately make Overload Check.

VENT DIE SCALING: L5-10 = 1d6 | L11-16 = 2d6 | L17-20 = 3d6

RST button = zero out on Discharge or end of combat.

features.name: Free Upcast (Codex P3)
features.description:
Mastery — Deepening the Connection (Page 3).

USES: 2/long rest at L11-16. (1 at L1-10. 3 at L17-20, each use may upcast by up to 2 levels.)

EFFECT: Upcast any spell by one level for free. Cannot exceed a spell level for which Syric has slots. The original non-upcast slot is consumed.

EXAMPLE: Cast Fireball at 4th level for free even if all 4th-level slots are used — but a 3rd-level slot must be available and is consumed.

4.title: Runic Flow — Magic as Natural Extension

features.name: Seamless Casting (Codex P4 NO V/S)
features.description:
Runic Flow — Magic as Natural Extension (Page 4).

USES: 2/long rest at L11-16. (1 at L1-10. 3 at L17-20.)

EFFECT: Cast one spell from one of Syric's inscribed runes without verbal or somatic components. Material components remain normal — a spellcasting focus covers non-costly materials; costly or consumed materials still required. Spell otherwise resolves normally.

NOTE: Does not count as a strain effect. Does not change slot economy. Can share a round with other actions under normal limits.

5.title: The Price of Power — Codex Surge

features.name: Echoed Rune (Codex P5)
features.description:
The Price of Power — Echoed Rune (Page 5).

USES: 1/long rest at L13. (2 at L15. 3 at L17.)

TRIGGER: After Syric casts an inscribed rune spell that damages at least one creature.

ACTIVATION: Bonus action after original spell resolves.

COST: Spend one spell slot equal to half the original slot level rounded down (min 1st-level), PLUS Codex Dice equal to the original spell's level. If the original spell was a cantrip, no slot cost.

EFFECT: No new save. No new attack roll. Auto-deals half the original spell's rolled damage (rounded down) to same targets. Uses original damage type.

EXAMPLE: Spell deals 40 damage → Echo deals 20 damage automatically.

item_charges.name: Codex Dice (Codex P5)
item_charges.description:
The Price of Power — Codex Surge (Page 5).

POOL: 10 Codex Dice at L13-16. (6 at L5-8, 8 at L9-12, 12 at L17-19, 14 at L20.) Regain all after long rest.

TRIGGER: After casting an inscribed rune spell that damages one or more creatures.

FREE ACTION (d6): No action cost. Spend up to proficiency bonus dice (5 at L13).
BONUS ACTION (d12): Dice become d12s. No limit on dice used.

Damage type matches the spell. Does not work with scrolls.

6.title: Duality of Runes — Veil and Reveal

features.name: Veil (Codex P6)
features.description:
Duality of Runes — Veil (Page 6).

USES: 2/long rest (half PB rounded down; PB=5 at L13).

COST: 1d12 psychic damage to hide runes. If kept hidden past 4 hours, 1d12 psychic again per additional 4 hours.

DURATION: 4 hours, then runes reveal unless extended.

EFFECT WHILE VEILED:
• Runes do not visibly glow. Normal sight cannot see rune flare.
• Detect Magic senses magic but not exact rune locations.
• Truesight / True Seeing still sees runes.
• +10 to Stealth checks (as Pass Without Trace).
• Advantage on first spell attack roll, OR impose disadvantage on first save against one of Syric's spell save DCs.

features.name: Reveal (Codex P6)
features.description:
Duality of Runes — Reveal (Page 6).

USES: 1/short rest.

ACTIVATION: Free action. Intentionally reveal hidden runes, causing them to visibly glow.

EFFECT: Syric may use his Arcana skill as the modifier for one Intimidation or Persuasion check. The sudden display unsettles, impresses, or unnerves the target.

7.title: Runic Resurgence — Deathline Reflex and Spell Recall

features.name: Deathline Reflex (Codex P7)
features.description:
Runic Resurgence — Deathline Reflex (Page 7).

USES: 1/long rest at L13. (2 at L15. 3 at L17.)

TRIGGER: When Syric drops below half HP from damage.

ACTIVATION: Reaction. Roll 1d4:

1 — RUNIC GUARD
Gain temporary HP = 2d6 + wizard level.

2 — SHADOW REPOSITION
Teleport up to 60 ft to a visible space. Gain resistance to all damage until start of next turn.

3 — PAIN CONVERSION
Gain resistance to triggering damage type for 1 minute, including the triggering hit.

4 — SPELL CLUTCH
Gain advantage on saves against triggering effect type for 1 minute (e.g., Spells, Piercing, Radiant).

features.name: Spell Recall (Codex P7)
features.description:
Runic Resurgence — Spell Recall (Page 7).

USES: 1/long rest at L13. (2 at L15. 3 at L17. Recalled spell does NOT become prepared.)

ACTIVATION: No action to recall. Recalled spell cast using normal casting time.

REQUIREMENTS: Must be a spell Syric knows. Must be a level Syric can cast. Must NOT have been prepared that day. Must have an available spell slot of that level or higher.

COST: Spell slot spent normally. Syric takes psychic damage equal to the spell level.

EXAMPLE: Syric knows Forcecage but did not prepare it. Uses Spell Recall, spends L7 slot, takes 7 psychic damage.

8.title: Advanced Inscription — Hybrid Sigils and Resonance Shaping

features.name: Hybrid Sigil (Codex P8)
features.description:
Advanced Inscription — Hybrid Sigil (Page 8).

USES: 1/long rest at L13. (2 at L15. 3 at L18.)

TRIGGER: When Syric casts a spell using a slot. Activate and choose one Hybrid Variant. Runic Interference is the exception — uses its own reaction trigger.

ARCANA CHECK: DC = 10 + slot level. Success: works. Failure: spell casts normally, Variant fails. Failure by 5+: also deals force damage = 2x slot level to Syric.

HYBRID VARIANTS:

RUNIC WARD — As part of casting.
One visible ally within 30 ft (60 ft at L17): +2 AC for 1d4 rounds, temp HP = 2d6 + wizard level, advantage on one d20 within next minute.

RUNIC MOMENTUM — As part of casting.
After spell resolves: Syric teleports up to 60 ft to unoccupied visible space, OR one willing visible ally within 60 ft moves up to 15 ft without opportunity attacks.

SPELL FUSION — As part of casting. Timing: can be added after outcome of save or attack roll is known.
Fuse a second inscribed spell. Cost: Resonance Points = combined levels of both spells. Primary spell (higher level) controls all targeting/area/attack roll/saves. Secondary contributes ONE property only: Damage, Defense, Movement, Condition, or Utility.
SECONDARY PROPERTIES: Damage — add secondary spell's damage dice. Defense — add one AC, resistance, or temp HP effect. Movement — add one movement/teleport/forced movement. Condition — add one condition. Utility — add one non-damaging utility effect (e.g. invisibility, silence).
HARD LIMITS: No second save. No second attack roll. No extra targets. No extra area. No summons. No created objects. No ongoing zones unless primary already creates one. Must be two different spells.
Creature must be affected by primary spell to be affected by secondary property.

RUNIC INTERFERENCE — Reaction.
Trigger: visible creature within 150 ft takes an action. Roll 1d6:
1 — Reaction Break: creature cannot take reactions until start of Syric's next turn.
2 — Arcane Trap: creature takes 3d8 + spell save DC force damage if it moves before Syric's next turn.
3 — Attack Distortion: all enemy's next d20 roll made with disadvantage.
4 — Save Distortion: Syric and all allies gain advantage on next d20 roll.
5 — Arcane Snare: enemy movement speed reduced to 0 until start of Syric's next turn.
6 — Runic Fatigue: enemy can only take 1 Action OR Bonus Action OR use movement until Syric's next turn.

item_charges.name: Resonance Points (Codex P8)
item_charges.description:
Advanced Inscription — Resonance Points (Page 8).

POOL: 12 points at L13 (half wizard level rounded up + INT mod = 7+5). Regain all after long rest.

Only one Resonance Technique per spell. Codex Surge can stack with one.

TECHNIQUES:
Split Rune — Cost: spell level (cantrip=1). Target second creature with same single-target spell.
Sculpted Rune — Cost: spell level. Choose up to PB creatures in area to auto-succeed and take no damage.
Transmuted Rune — Cost: spell level. Change damage type to DM-approved type.
Weighted Rune — Cost: 1 RP per die. Reroll damage dice up to INT mod, must use new rolls.
Silent Ledger — Cost: spell level. Remove verbal and somatic components from one rune spell.

features.name: Arcane Devouring (Codex P9)
features.description:
Arcane Devouring — Embodied Relic (Page 9). RITUAL — tap to reference.

REQUIREMENT: Level 15+ or Codex page unlocked. Must possess and be attuned (if required) to the item.

RITUAL TIME: 8 hours uninterrupted. Item is destroyed at the end regardless of outcome.

EFFECT ON SUCCESS: Syric absorbs all magical properties as Living Rune functions. Powers keep original activation type, action cost, charges, recharge timing, and restrictions unless DM modifies them. Body and runes replace the item as point of use. Save DC/attack bonus: use the better of item's or Syric's.

INTERNAL ATTUNEMENT SLOTS: Syric gains 1 bonus internal attunement slot (RAW slots +1). If devoured item required attunement, it occupies 1 Internal Attunement Slot. No-attunement items do not occupy a slot — Syric may devour any number, but normal stacking rules apply. Consumables (potions, scrolls, single-use charms) are not valid targets unless DM allows.

ARCANA CHECK DC: Common 10 | Uncommon 12 | Rare 14 | Very Rare 16 | Legendary 20 | Artifact 28.
+2 DC if sentient. +2 DC if cursed. +3 DC if charged.
Advantage: attuned 7+ days, OR used in major story moment. Disadvantage: never used.

OUTCOMES:
SUCCESS: Absorb all properties. Attunement items use 1 Internal Attunement Slot.
FAILURE: No benefit. Take force/psychic damage = 5x rarity tier (Common 5, Uncommon 10, Rare 15, Very Rare 20, Legendary 25, Artifact 30).
FAILURE BY 5+: As failure + 1 exhaustion + one devoured property unavailable until long rest.
NAT 1: As failure + one devoured no-attunement property permanently expelled. If none, one Internal Attunement Slot power unavailable for 7 days.

CAST TO THE NETHER (1-hour ritual): Permanently release one absorbed relic pattern. Power is lost forever — original item does not reform. Syric takes force damage = 10x rarity tier in a 20-ft radius. No save DC and no immunities negate this damage. Internal Attunement Slot freed at end of ritual.

EXAMPLES:
Staff of the Magi: Destroyed, occupies 1 Internal Attunement Slot. Spells, charges, spell absorption, and all properties become internal rune functions using normal charge rules.
Ring of the Twilight Mind: Destroyed, dual concentration property becomes internal. If released later, the ability is gone and the ring does not return.

features.name: Rune Mastery (Codex P10)
features.description:
Ascension — Rune Mastery (Page 10). Daily pick — mark used when today's choice has been made.

Choose ONE option each long rest:
OPTION A: Pick THREE spells of level 3 or lower — treat as cantrips for the day.
OPTION B: Pick ONE spell of level 4-7 — treat as a cantrip for the day.

features.name: Ascendant Rune Casting (Codex P10)
features.description:
Ascension — Ascendant Rune Casting (Page 10). PASSIVE — always active once page is found.

COMPONENT RULE: Inscribed rune spells require no verbal, somatic, or non-costly material components. Costly or consumed components still required.

RUNE VISIBILITY: Runes muted by default. Only visible via Truesight, True Seeing, or features that specifically reveal hidden magical markings. Detect Magic can sense them with a successful Arcana check vs Syric's spell save DC.

EXHAUSTION: No longer gains exhaustion from normal Codex spellcasting strain. Can still gain from Hybrid Sigil failures, Overload, failed rituals, and normal adventuring.

SOCIAL PENALTY: Disadvantage on Charisma checks with common mundane NPCs if deathless/rune nature is obvious. No penalty if runes are muted and Syric appears normal.

RUNE MASTERY (daily pick — mark used when today's choice has been made):
OPTION A: Pick THREE spells of level 3 or lower — treat as cantrips for the day.
OPTION B: Pick ONE spell of level 4-7 — treat as a cantrip for the day.

Codex DIE UPGRADE:
Codex Dice become d8. Any 1 or 2 rolled becomes a 3.
MAXIMIZED DICE: One damage die per spell level is maximized. Example: 5th-level spell with 10 Codex Dice — 5 dice are automatically 8s, remainder rolled normally.
